No Poverty.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to eradicating poverty or implementing social protection systems.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to ending hunger, improving nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to health outcomes, disease prevention, or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a required undergraduate program (Target 4.3) focused on 'sharpening students’ quantitative modeling skills' for employment in analytics and AI, directly supporting Target 4.4.
evidence: analytics and artificial intelligence (AI) has been shaking many industries

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course enhances quantitative skills for strategic and operational decisions in functions like production and finance, directly supporting higher economic productivity and innovation (Target 8.2) and productive activities (Target 8.3).
evidence: influences on how firms make strategic, tactic, and operational decisions

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on 'analytics and artificial intelligence (AI)' and 'Management Science models' directly enhances scientific research and technological capabilities in industrial sectors, promoting innovation (Target 9.5).
evidence: Management Science models and solution approaches

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to reducing income or social inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to urban planning, housing, or sustainable communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course teaches optimization models applied to 'production' decisions. These quantitative skills can lead to more efficient resource use and waste reduction, aligning with Targets 12.2 and 12.5.
evidence: Management Science models and solution approaches
operational decisions for different functions including... production

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or resilience.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives directly relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course lists 'Social responsibility and ethics' as a core competency. This indirectly supports developing individuals who can contribute to effective and accountable institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds capacity in 'analytics and artificial intelligence (AI)' and 'information technology,' which are enabling technologies supporting development goals and capacity-building (Targets 17.8, 17.9).
